name: hexa-native
description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block for `.py` / `.sh` / `.c` writes inside any project rooted at a directory containing a `project.tape` marker (sidecar's canonical project identity file), implemented in hexa-lang (`_hexa_native.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.4.0 adds `.c` to the blocked set (alongside `.py` / `.sh`) — a hexa-native project takes `.hexa` only; hand-written C is refused (the C bootstrap is regenerated via `hexa cc --regen`, never hand-edited per commons @D g61). Covers BOTH write channels: (1) structured file tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit via file_path); (2) the Bash redirect channel — `> X.c`, `>> X.sh`, heredoc-to-redirect (`cat > X.py << EOF`), `tee [-a] X`, `dd of=X`, `cp`/`mv` dest, with relative targets resolved against the payload `cwd`. Quote-aware scan; fd-dup (`2>&1`) yields no target; non-write Bash (`python X.py`, `cat X.c`, `gcc X.c`, `chmod X.sh`) passes through. Walks up parent dirs of the target; if `project.tape` is found, the write is denied with a message stating the same logic IS writable here as `.hexa`. `.c` matches only the literal `.c` suffix (not `.cc` / `.cpp` / `.cxx` / `.h`). NO opt-out: no env var, no config file, no exception list, no self-exclusion — uninstall the plugin if you need a way out. Targets only `.py` / `.sh` / `.c`; other extensions pass through. Projects without `project.tape` are unaffected.
